title: Modules, Packages, Environments, and Command Lines · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Turn the reporting pipeline into an installable and repeatable command-line tool. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an installable data-report command. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain module search, import caching, names, qualified access, and circular-import failure conditions.
Organize public module functions, constants, docstrings, and private implementation names around stable responsibilities.
Use the main guard to separate importable behavior from command-line execution and testing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-basics cumulative stakeholder program."""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

# Add one tested, typed, inspectable program increment per activity.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Python Argparse Cli applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Build a command-line interface with argparse subcommands, validated options, useful help, standard streams, and exit codes.
